title: Revolve Q1 2026 Earnings Call Summary
text: Revolve delivered strong Q1 2026 results with net sales of $343 million, representing 16% year-over-year growth—the company's highest growth rate in nearly four years. Net income reached $14 million with diluted EPS of $0.20, up 25% year-over-year. Key Metrics: Active Customers grew 8% to 2.9 million (trailing twelve-month). Gross Margin expanded 68 basis points to 52.7%. Free Cash Flow surged 95% year-over-year to $45 million. Cash Position increased $33 million sequentially to $336 million with zero debt. Strategic Initiatives: Revolve Los Angeles launched as the company's first namesake brand, generating more than 200 million impressions through premium advertising featuring Bella Hadid. March saw a more than 40% increase in Revolve search interest year-over-year. GrowGood Beauty partnership with Cardi B exceeded expectations, with products selling out in less than an hour at presale and official launch. The brand rapidly accumulated 640,000 Instagram followers. Physical Retail: Miami flagship store signed; Aspen and Los Angeles locations showing positive cross-channel synergies with local ecommerce sales lifts. Headwinds: CFO Jesse Timmermans noted input cost pressures from higher freight and materials impacting margin, particularly affecting the REVOLVE segment. The Middle East experienced meaningful geopolitical slowdown continuing into Q2.
